Input validation must be performed to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Output encoding must be implemented to prevent XSS attacks.
Store passwords securely using hashing algorithms with salts to prevent brute force attacks.
Implement CAPTCHA or rate limiting to prevent automated brute force attacks.
Use prepared statements and parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Regularly update and patch software to address security vulnerabilities.
Implement secure communication protocols like HTTPS to protect data in transit.
Apply least privilege principle to limit access rights for users and processes.
Implement security headers to protect against common web vulnerabilities.
Regularly conduct security audits and penetration testing to identify and address security weaknesses.
